subject: kerning adjustments in text rendering
old: old kerning behavior (under-corrected kerning, factor=6)
new: correct kerning (factor=0)
truth: Kerning adjustments now correctly apply kerning; to restore old under-corrected behavior set rcParams['text.kerning_factor'] to 6 instead of 0.
quote: Due to an error in how kerning adjustments were applied, previous versions of Matplotlib would under-correct kerning. This version will now correctly apply kerning (for fonts supported by FreeType). To restore the old behavior (e.g., for test images), you may set :rc:`text.kerning_factor` to 6 (instead of 0).

subject: Axes creation methods (pyplot.axes, Figure.add_axes, Figure.add_subplot)
old: Previously, these functions would detect if you were attempting to create Axes with the same keyword arguments as already-existing Axes in the current Figure, and if so, they would return the existing Axes.
new: Now, pyplot.axes, Figure.add_axes, and Figure.add_subplot will always create new Axes.
truth: Stop relying on pyplot.axes, Figure.add_axes, and Figure.add_subplot to return an existing Axes when keyword arguments match; they now always create a new Axes.
quote: In the past, these functions would detect if you were attempting to create Axes with the same keyword arguments as already-existing Axes in the current Figure, and if so, they would return the existing Axes. Now, .pyplot.axes, .figure.Figure.add_axes, and .figure.Figure.add_subplot will always create new Axes.

from_fact: previously, these functions would detect if you were attempting to create axes with the same keyword arguments as already-existing axes in the current figure, and if so, they would return the existing axes.
now, pyplot.axes, figure.add_axes, and figure.add_subplot will always create new axes.

subject: pyplot.gca and Figure.gca keyword argument behavior
old: Previously, gca accepted keyword arguments; if they matched an existing Axes, that Axes was returned, otherwise new Axes was created with those kwargs.
new: Now, keyword arguments are only considered if there are no Axes at all in the current figure. In a future release, these functions will not accept keyword arguments at all.
truth: Stop passing keyword arguments to gca expecting them to match an existing Axes; kwargs are now only used when no Axes exist in the figure.
quote: In the past, these functions accepted keyword arguments. If the keyword arguments matched an already-existing Axes, then that Axes would be returned, otherwise new Axes would be created with those keyword arguments. Now, the keyword arguments are only considered if there are no Axes at all in the current figure.

from_fact: previously, gca accepted keyword arguments; if they matched an existing axes, that axes was returned, otherwise new axes was created with those kwargs.
now, keyword arguments are only considered if there are no axes at all in the current figure. in a future release, these functions will not accept keyword arguments at all.

subject: Bracket arrow styles angleA/angleB
old: angleA and angleB options on Bracket arrow styles (]-[, ]-, -[, |-|) were allowed but did nothing
new: angleA and angleB options on Bracket arrow styles are now applied and affect the arrow
truth: Bracket arrow styles (]-[, ]-, -[, |-|) now apply angleA and angleB; previously these parameters were silently ignored.
quote: Previously, the *angleA* and *angleB* options were allowed, but did

from_fact: anglea and angleb options on bracket arrow styles (]-[, ]-, -[, |-|) were allowed but did nothing
anglea and angleb options on bracket arrow styles are now applied and affect the arrow

subject: pcolormesh
old: snapping disabled by default (grid boundary lines visible with transparency)
new: snapping enabled by default (grid boundary lines disappear)
truth: pcolormesh now applies snapping by default, removing grid boundary lines visible with transparency; set rcParams['pcolormesh.snap'] = False to restore old behavior.
quote: This version now applies snapping by default. To restore the old behavior (e.g., for test images), you may set :rc:`pcolormesh.snap` to `False`.

from_fact: snapping disabled by default (grid boundary lines visible with transparency)
snapping enabled by default (grid boundary lines disappear)

subject: Colormap.set_bad/set_under/set_over
old: Modifying colormap in place with set_bad, set_under, set_over
new: Use Colormap.with_extremes() to copy first then set extremes
truth: Stop using set_bad/set_under/set_over directly on builtin colormaps because they modify in place; use Colormap.with_extremes(bad=..., under=..., over=...) instead which copies first.
quote: Because the .Colormap.set_bad, .Colormap.set_under and .Colormap.set_over methods modify the colormap in place, the user must be careful to first make a copy of the colormap if setting the extreme colors e.g. for a builtin colormap. The new Colormap.with_extremes(bad=..., under=..., over=...) can be used to first copy the colormap and set the extreme colors on that copy.

from_fact: modifying colormap in place with set_bad, set_under, set_over
use colormap.with_extremes() to copy first then set extremes

subject: Colormap.set_extremes
old: No set_extremes method existed
new: Colormap.set_extremes() is new but suffers same in-place mutation issue as individual setters
truth: Colormap.set_extremes() modifies the colormap in place just like set_bad/set_under/set_over, so you must copy first; prefer Colormap.with_extremes() which copies automatically.
quote: The new .Colormap.set_extremes method is provided for API symmetry with .Colormap.with_extremes, but note that it suffers from the same issue as the earlier individual setters.

from_fact: colormap.set_extremes() is new but suffers same in-place mutation issue as individual setters

subject: xtick.color rcParam
old: xtick.color controlled both tick color and label color
new: xtick.labelcolor now controls label color independently; xtick.color only controls tick color
truth: Stop using xtick.color to set tick label color; use xtick.labelcolor instead, which defaults to 'inherit' from xtick.color.
quote: The label color can now be set independently using :rc:`xtick.labelcolor`.

from_fact: xtick.color controlled both tick color and label color
xtick.labelcolor now controls label color independently; xtick.color only controls tick color

subject: ytick.color rcParam
old: ytick.color controlled both tick color and label color
new: ytick.labelcolor now controls label color independently; ytick.color only controls tick color
truth: Stop using ytick.color to set tick label color; use ytick.labelcolor instead, which defaults to 'inherit' from ytick.color.
quote: The label color can now be set independently using :rc:`xtick.labelcolor`.

from_fact: ytick.color controlled both tick color and label color
ytick.labelcolor now controls label color independently; ytick.color only controls tick color

subject: WebAgg backend mouse button reporting
old: WebAgg reported middle-click events as right clicks
new: WebAgg now reports the correct button type for middle-click events
truth: WebAgg backend now correctly reports middle-click events instead of misreporting them as right clicks; code that relied on the old wrong mapping will break.
quote: Formerly, WebAgg would report middle-click events as right clicks, but now reports the correct button type.

from_fact: webagg reported middle-click events as right clicks
webagg now reports the correct button type for middle-click events

subject: Font subsetting in PDF/PS output
old: Font subsetting used embedded ttconv C code
new: Font subsetting re-written in Python; some composite characters and outlines may have changed slightly
truth: Font subsetting in PDF and PostScript has been re-written from C to Python, which may slightly alter some composite characters and outlines in the output.
quote: Font subsetting in PDF and PostScript has been re-written from the embedded ttconv C code to Python. Some composite characters and outlines may have changed slightly.

from_fact: font subsetting used embedded ttconv c code
font subsetting re-written in python; some composite characters and outlines may have changed slightly

subject: ArrowStyle parameters
old: beginarrow and endarrow parameters
new: arrow parameter
truth: Stop using beginarrow and endarrow parameters in ArrowStyle; use the new arrow parameter with arrow strings like '<-', ']-[', ']->' instead.
quote: The new *arrow* parameter to `.ArrowStyle` substitutes the use of the

subject: colormap access API
old: matplotlib.cm.get_cmap
new: matplotlib.colormaps (item access)
truth: Stop using matplotlib.cm.get_cmap and use matplotlib.colormaps (or plt.colormaps) with item access instead.
quote: We recommend to use the new API instead of the `~.cm.get_cmap` and `~.cm.register_cmap` functions for new code. `matplotlib.cm.get_cmap` and `matplotlib.cm.register_cmap` will eventually be deprecated and removed.

subject: colormap registration API
old: matplotlib.cm.register_cmap
new: matplotlib.colormaps.register()
truth: Stop using matplotlib.cm.register_cmap and use matplotlib.colormaps.register() instead.
quote: We recommend to use the new API instead of the `~.cm.get_cmap` and `~.cm.register_cmap` functions for new code. `matplotlib.cm.get_cmap` and `matplotlib.cm.register_cmap` will eventually be deprecated and removed.

subject: Axes3D.plot_surface
old: plot_surface ignored masked arrays and NaNs, rendering quads even with invalid data
new: plot_surface now hides quads that contain masked or NaN points
truth: plot_surface now hides quads that contain masked or NaN points, similar to contour with corner_mask=True
quote: hide quads that contain masked or NaN points.

from_fact: plot_surface ignored masked arrays and nans, rendering quads even with invalid data
plot_surface now hides quads that contain masked or nan points

subject: Qt4 backend support
old: Qt4 backends (Qt4Agg, Qt4Cairo)
truth: Stop using Qt4 backends; they have been removed. Use Qt5 or Qt6 backends (QtAgg, QtCairo, Qt5Agg, Qt5Cairo) instead.
quote: Support for Qt4 has been dropped.

subject: WebAgg backend timer framework
old: Tornado-based timer support
new: asyncio-based timer support by default
truth: WebAgg backend now defaults to asyncio instead of Tornado for timer support; code relying on Tornado-specific timer behaviour may break.
quote: The WebAgg backend defaults to using asyncio over Tornado for timer support.

subject: rcParams text.antialiased timing
old: Setting rcParams['text.antialiased'] in a context manager after figure creation affected the saved output
new: Setting rcParams['text.antialiased'] in a context manager after figure creation has no effect; the rcParam must be set before figure creation
truth: rcParams['text.antialiased'] must be set before creating the figure; setting it in a context manager after figure creation is now a no-op.
quote: previously this had an effect, now this is a no-op

from_fact: setting rcparams['text.antialiased'] in a context manager after figure creation affected the saved output
setting rcparams['text.antialiased'] in a context manager after figure creation has no effect; the rcparam must be set before figure creation

subject: label_outer() method
old: label_outer() only removed ticklabels, leaving tick lines visible
new: label_outer() now has remove_inner_ticks parameter (default behavior not specified, but new parameter exists)
truth: label_outer() now has a remove_inner_ticks parameter; pass remove_inner_ticks=True to also remove inner tick lines, otherwise behavior may differ from before
quote: Up to now, label_outer() has only removed the ticklabels. The ticks lines were left visible. This is now configurable through a new parameter label_outer(remove_inner_ticks=True).

from_fact: label_outer() only removed ticklabels, leaving tick lines visible
label_outer() now has remove_inner_ticks parameter (default behavior not specified, but new parameter exists)

subject: legend shadow parameter
old: shadow parameter only accepted booleans
new: shadow parameter now also accepts dicts with Patch keywords
truth: legend shadow parameter now accepts dicts in addition to booleans; dicts can contain any keywords for patches.Patch
quote: The *shadow* parameter of legends now accepts dicts in addition to booleans.

from_fact: shadow parameter now also accepts dicts with patch keywords

subject: Axes.pcolor return type
old: returns flattened PolyCollection with only valid (unmasked) polygons
new: returns PolyQuadMesh (subclass of PolyCollection)
truth: Axes.pcolor now returns a PolyQuadMesh instead of a flattened PolyCollection.
quote: Axes.pcolor previously returned a flattened .PolyCollection with only the valid polygons (unmasked) contained within it. Now, we return a .PolyQuadMesh

from_fact: returns flattened polycollection with only valid (unmasked) polygons

subject: Shadow patch default shade
old: shadow darkness was fixed (implicit default)
new: default shade is 0.7
truth: The Shadow patch now has a default shade of 0.7; previously the shadow darkness was fixed and not configurable.
quote: The .Shadow patch now has a *shade* argument to control the shadow darkness. If 1, the shadow is black, if 0, the shadow has the same color as the patch that is shadowed. The default value, which earlier was fixed, is 0.7.

subject: Contour plots label format
old: old default label format (likely %1.3f or similar)
new: ScalarFormatter
truth: Contour plots now default to using ScalarFormatter instead of the old default label format; pass fmt="%1.3f" to restore old behavior.
quote: Pass ``fmt="%1.3f"`` to the contouring call to restore the old default label

from_fact: old default label format (likely %1.3f or similar)

subject: Axes.spines
old: OrderedDict
new: dedicated Spines container class
truth: Axes.spines is now a Spines container class instead of an OrderedDict; attribute-style access (ax.spines.top) is supported but dict-like access still works.
quote: ``Axes.spines`` is now a dedicated container class `.Spines` for a set of

subject: Collection color mapping with set_array(None)
old: Calling Collection.set_array(None) had no effect
new: Calling Collection.set_array(None) now turns off color mapping
truth: Calling Collection.set_array(None) now turns off color mapping; previously it had no effect.
quote: Color mapping can be turned off by calling ``Collection.set_array(None)``.

from_fact: calling collection.set_array(none) now turns off color mapping

subject: Collection edgecolor='face' with facecolor='none' and mappable array set
old: When a mappable array was set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', edges were color-mapped
new: When a mappable array is set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', both faces and edges are left uncolored
truth: When a mappable array is set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', edges are now left uncolored instead of being color-mapped.
quote: ``edgecolor='face'``, both the faces and the edges are left uncolored.

from_fact: when a mappable array was set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', edges were color-mapped
when a mappable array is set with facecolor='none' and edgecolor='face', both faces and edges are left uncolored

subject: subplots sharex sharey tick label visibility
old: When sharex=True, x tick labels are hidden for axes not in the first column; when sharey=True, y tick labels are hidden for axes not in the last row.
new: Visibility is now adjusted based on rcParams xtick.labeltop, xtick.labelbottom, ytick.labelleft, ytick.labelright — labels are hidden on the first row and last column as appropriate.
truth: subplots(sharex=True, sharey=True) now respects rcParams for tick label positioning when deciding which tick labels to hide.
quote: Shared-axes ``subplots`` tick label visibility is now correct for top or left labels

from_fact: when sharex=true, x tick labels are hidden for axes not in the first column; when sharey=true, y tick labels are hidden for axes not in the last row.
visibility is now adjusted based on rcparams xtick.labeltop, xtick.labelbottom, ytick.labelleft, ytick.labelright — labels are hidden on the first row and last column as appropriate.
